markdown.markdown:
::: {.cv-map-wrap} ::: {.cv-map} Open CV PDF
Current Position
**Postdoctoral Research Associate** Steward Observatory, University of Arizona Current research centers on theory and simulations of star formation, with interests spanning the interstellar medium, molecular clouds, filaments, angular momentum transport, MHD simulations, and radiative transfer or synthetic-observation methods.
Education
### Education & Academic Trajectory - **2013–2018** — **BSc in Physics**, Universidad Veracruzana, Mexico - **2018–2020** — **MSc in Astrophysics**, IRyA-UNAM, Mexico - **2020–2024** — **PhD in Astrophysics**, IRyA-UNAM, Mexico - **2024–present** — **Postdoctoral Research Associate**, University of Arizona
Publications
### Selected Publications - **2026** — *On the role of gravity, turbulence, and the magnetic field in angular momentum transfer within molecular clouds* - **2026** — *Effect of gravity-driven longitudinal flows on angular momentum transport in filaments* - **2025** — Comparison between TS and GHC molecular-cloud models - **2022** — Angular momentum during gravitational fragmentation of molecular clouds - **2020** — Neutron and quark star constraints using GW170817
Talks
### Talks & Conferences Recent presentations include contributed and invited talks on angular momentum in molecular clouds and filaments, star formation simulations, and longitudinal flows across meetings in Mexico, Ireland, France, Australia, Canada, Italy, the United States, and Colombia.
Awards
### Awards & Recognition - **2026** — Nominated for the **Postdoc-as-Mentor Award** at the University of Arizona - Multiple academic stages completed with **honorific mention**
Outreach
### Outreach Long-term participation in public astronomy outreach includes repeated involvement in **Noche de las Estrellas** in Mexico, as well as design and workshop activities connected to eclipse outreach events.
Service
### Professional Service Professional service includes peer review for **The Astrophysical Journal (ApJ)** and the **Open Journal of Astrophysics (OJA)**.
::: :::
